name: Skilled Nursing Facility Records (CMS aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with CMS Conditions of Participation for Long-Term Care Facilities and the MDS 3.0 RAI process.

data.text: Each resident has an MDS 3.0 comprehensive assessment within 14 days of admission, with quarterly and annual reassessments.
An interdisciplinary care plan is developed within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment, reviewed and revised after each assessment and on change of condition.
Documentation supports F-tag compliance (e.g. F584 dignity, F689 falls, F694 ADLs, F758 unnecessary drugs).
Incidents are documented, investigated, and reported per CMS abuse / neglect / theft requirements.
Medication administration follows facility policy: prescribing, administration, reconciliation. Controlled substances follow DEA storage and recording rules.
Resident rights, advance directives, and surrogate decision-makers are documented and accessible to the care team.
Records are retained for at least 5 years from discharge per the federal CMS minimum, longer where state law requires.

clauses.title: MDS 3.0 assessment cycle
clauses.body: Each resident must have an MDS 3.0 comprehensive assessment within 14 days of admission, with quarterly and annual reassessments.

clauses.title: Interdisciplinary care plan
clauses.body: An interdisciplinary care plan must be developed within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment and revised on change of condition.

clauses.title: F-tag documentation
clauses.body: Documentation must support F-tag compliance (e.g. dignity, falls, ADLs, unnecessary drugs).

clauses.title: Incident investigation + reporting
clauses.body: Incidents must be documented, investigated, and reported per CMS abuse / neglect / theft requirements.

clauses.title: Medication administration recorded
clauses.body: Prescribing, administration, and reconciliation of medication must be recorded; controlled substances follow DEA storage and recording rules.

clauses.title: Resident rights + advance directives
clauses.body: Resident rights, advance directives, and surrogate decision-makers must be documented and accessible.

clauses.title: 5-year federal retention
clauses.body: Records must be retained at least 5 years from discharge per the federal CMS minimum; longer where state law requires.
